finding: bug_010 — BottomSheetRouter chained route replace stalls (second sheet content invisible, B4 cartel-scanner flow broken)
root_cause: No `key` prop on `<BottomSheetContainer>` in BottomSheetRouter.tsx → React reuses the same instance across the `closing(prev) → opening(queued)` route flip. The container's mount-only entrance `useEffect` (deps `[]`) never re-fires for the new route, stale Animated.Values remain at (opacity=0, translateY=40), and `OPEN_DONE` is never dispatched — reducer wedges at `opening(queued)`.
fix_summary: Single-line addition `key={state.route}` on `<BottomSheetContainer>` in BottomSheetRouter.tsx (router file) — forces unmount/remount when the route id flips. Inline JSDoc traçabilité bug_010 (9 lines) explains the failure mode + names the RED test file.

correctness.summary: Fix is causally correct. The `key={state.route}` placement on the JSX element (line 151) is the minimum React idiom that forces reconciliation to discard the previous fiber and mount a new one when the route id changes. Confirms the failure model: BottomSheetContainer's entrance effect runs on mount only (deps `[]`), so route swap without remount → stale Animated.Values → no OPEN_DONE. Diff shows ONLY the `key` prop + 9-line JSDoc; no other behavioural mutation. Test #1 asserts the observable post-condition: `getBottomSheetState().kind === 'open'` after the chained replace settles — exactly the wedge symptom. Test #3 walks the full state machine `opening(A) → open(A) → closing(A) → opening(B) → open(B)` and asserts each transition after `runAllTimers()`. Test #2 counts `Animated.parallel` invocations (1 entrance + 1 exit + 1 entrance = 3) — proxy for the entrance effect firing on the remounted container. 3/3 chained-replace tests PASS, 44/44 bottom-sheet-router suite PASS. Full FE suite 2704/2704 PASS.

scope-fidelity.summary: Surgical scope: 1 line of production code (`key={state.route}`) + 9 lines of comment in BottomSheetRouter.tsx, + 175 lines of new RED test file `chained-replace.test.tsx`. ZERO touched in BottomSheetContainer.tsx / bottomSheetMachine.ts / bottomSheetStore.ts / routes.ts. No new dependency. No alternative refactor (e.g. converting the entrance effect to depend on `route`) — fix targets the actual root cause at the boundary. Pure additive on test side, mono-line on source side.

kiss-dry-hexagonal.summary: Minimal idiomatic React. `key={state.route}` is the canonical fix for `force-remount-on-discriminator-change` (documented React pattern). DRY: reuses existing `test-harness.tsx` (installMockRoutes, RouterTestHost, RouterHandle), no harness duplication. Pure timer-based determinism (jest.useFakeTimers + runAllTimers) — no `waitFor` polling, no race. JSDoc names the symptom + file + acceptance criterion (bug_010 / B4 cartel-scanner / chained-replace.test.tsx) → future readers find the test from the code and vice-versa.

a11y-design-system.summary: N/A (no UI surface or token change). The fix preserves the announce-once-per-route logic (announcedRouteRef compares against `state.route`, unaffected by container instance identity). Container remount on key flip means the `accessibilityLabel` prop is re-applied fresh for the new route — no stale a11y announcement leakage. Score reflects 'no regression' rather than uplift.

security-honesty.summary: UFR-013 honesty respected: JSDoc explicitly states the bug (not euphemized), names the broken flow (B4 cartel-scanner), points at the RED test. Tests use observable effects (reducer state via `getBottomSheetState()`, `Animated.parallel` call count) rather than introspecting opaque `Animated.Value._value` private — aligns with the opaque-Animated-Value contract (RN does not expose `_value` as public API). No TODO/FIXME, no eslint-disable, no feature flag introduced (UFR-015). No PII surface change. Test #2 introspects an implementation detail (Animated.parallel invocation count) — flagged as ACCEPTABLE_WITH_NOTE below.

test2_parallel_count_proxy.evidence: Test #2 spies on `Animated.parallel` and asserts call count === 3 across the chained replace. This couples the test to BottomSheetContainer's choice to wrap entrance+exit in `Animated.parallel` rather than `Animated.sequence` or separate `Animated.timing` calls. If a future refactor (B6+) switches the container to e.g. `Animated.sequence` or factors the entrance into a custom hook that uses `useNativeDriver` differently, this test would break — but the production behaviour might still be correct. NOTE: this is the LEAST-FRAGILE observable available without exposing `Animated.Value._value` (which is not part of the public RN API and tests calling it would be incorrect per the opaque-Animated-Value contract). Tests #1 and #3 cover the same fix via reducer state — Test #2 is redundant-but-load-bearing-on-the-WHY. Accept for V1: it documents the causal mechanism (entrance effect must re-fire on remount). Track as backlog if Animated.parallel disappears: 'feat(test): swap parallel-spy for entrance-effect counter once BottomSheetContainer exposes a test hook'.

no_opacity_value_test.evidence: Tests do NOT introspect `Animated.Value._value` for opacity / translateY. Rationale: `_value` is a private RN API (underscore prefix). Asserting on it would couple the test to RN internals and violate the opaque-Animated-Value contract. The fact that the reducer reaches `open(queued)` (test #1, #3) is a necessary post-condition of OPEN_DONE being dispatched, which in turn is a necessary post-condition of the entrance Animated.parallel completing — so the chain `parallel completes → OPEN_DONE → state.kind === 'open'` is fully covered transitively. Visual verification (E2E Maestro) would be required to validate actual opacity rendering, but that is out of unit-test scope.

key_via_state_route_string.evidence: `key={state.route}` is a string id from a closed enum (`BottomSheetRouteId`). Cannot collide across replaces because the queued-route guard already prevents `open(A) → open(A)` chained replace without an explicit close. If a future scenario opens route A → closes → reopens route A back-to-back, React would reuse the previous fiber (key unchanged) — but that scenario is already gated by the `idle` reducer state interrupting the keyed identity (the `<BottomSheetContainer>` is unmounted when `state.kind === 'idle'` returns `null` at line 133), so the new mount starts fresh regardless. Fix is correct against the actual state machine.

gates.no_unicode_emoji: PASS — no emoji in router source or test source (grep `[\u{1F300}-\u{1FAFF}]` returns 0).
gates.bury_dead_code: PASS — purely additive (1 line key prop + JSDoc + new test file); no deletion needed.
gates.honesty_jsdoc: PASS — JSDoc explicitly names the bug, the broken flow, and the RED test file.

build_verification.lint: PASS — `cd museum-frontend && npm run lint` exit 0 (eslint --max-warnings=0 && tsc --noEmit).
build_verification.tests_chained_replace: PASS — `npx jest --testPathPattern=chained-replace` → 1 suite / 3 tests passed (Time 2.642s). All 3 RED tests now GREEN with the fix applied.
build_verification.tests_bottom_sheet_router_subtree: PASS — `npx jest --testPathPattern=bottom-sheet-router` → 9 suites / 44 tests passed (Time 4.424s). All pre-existing C4 tests (bottomSheetMachine, BottomSheetRouter, cartel-scanner-route, attachment-picker-route, focus-restore, integration, sequencing, swipe-down) intact.
build_verification.tests_full_suite: PASS — `npm test` → 249 suites / 2704 tests / 4 snapshots passed (Time 43.002s). Zero regression vs. baseline c899470fb (B1 final).

findings.title: Test #2 couples to Animated.parallel implementation detail
findings.recommendation: ACCEPT for V1. Tests #1 and #3 cover the fix via reducer state (the public contract). Test #2 adds causal traceability (entrance effect re-fired) at the cost of coupling to BottomSheetContainer's choice of `Animated.parallel`. If a later refactor changes that, swap the spy for a counter exposed by the container via a test hook. Non-blocking.

recommendation: APPROVED — commit + push OK. Fix is the minimum-causal-correction for bug_010, scope is surgical (1 production line), 3 RED tests now GREEN and lock the regression, ALL pre-existing C4 tests intact (44/44), full FE suite green (2704/2704), lint clean. One NICE_TO_HAVE finding (Animated.parallel coupling in test #2) — backlog only. Ready to merge into chat-ux-refonte branch.
